introduction: In Uttar Pradesh, marriage registrations are facilitated by the Stamp and Registration Department online via the IGRSUP portal. Aadhaar-based verification is tightly integrated, reducing manual paperwork significantly.
quickFacts:
Important to Know
  • Processing Time: Can be instant or take a few days post Aadhaar verification.
  • Application Fee: ₹10 for Hindu Marriage Act, ₹50 for Special Marriage Act. Additional late fees may apply.
  • A Marriage Certificate is valid for a lifetime.
documentsRequired:
Documents Required for Marriage Registration

Note: You need to provide documents for Aadhaar eKYC, Proof of Age, Photographs, and Witnesses.
Proof of Identity & Age
  • Aadhaar Card numbers (mandatory)
  • Marksheet or Birth Certificate
  • Passport
Proof of Address
  • Voter ID
  • Utility Bills
  • Aadhaar Card
Evidence of Marriage & Affidavit
  • Ceremony photos (couple in wedding attire)
  • Affidavit from couple
  • Wedding invitation
Witnesses
  • Two to three witnesses
  • Witness identity and address proofs
onlineApplication:
Method 1: Apply Online via IGRSUP Portal

Uttar Pradesh's IGRSUP (Inspector General of Registration, Stamps & Fees) portal offers Aadhaar e-KYC based online marriage registration — the fastest and most recommended route.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit the IGRSUP Portal
    Go to igrsup.gov.in. Click on "Marriage Registration" (विवाह पंजीकरण) from the home page.
  2. Click 'Apply' / 'आवेदन करें'
    Select "New Application". The portal is available in Hindi and English.
  3. Aadhaar e-KYC Verification
    Enter the Aadhaar number of the husband and verify via OTP. Then enter the Aadhaar number of the wife and verify. Basic details are auto-filled. Ensure your Aadhaar-linked mobile number is active.
  4. Fill Remaining Details
    Enter: date and place of marriage, tehsil, district, religion, and full details of 2 witnesses including their Aadhaar numbers and addresses.
  5. Upload Documents
    Upload scanned JPG/PDF copies (max 40 KB each): age proof (Birth Certificate/10th Marksheet), address proof (Aadhaar/Voter ID), photo of husband, photo of wife, joint wedding photo, and witness ID proofs.
  6. Pay Fee Online
    Pay ₹10 online via the IGRSUP payment gateway. Print the Application Slip with your reference number — mandatory for the SRO visit.
  7. Visit Sub-Registrar Office (SRO)
    Both spouses and witnesses must appear at the SRO of the area where the marriage was registered. Carry all originals. The Sub-Registrar verifies and registers the marriage.
Download Certificate: Once approved, download the Marriage Certificate directly from igrsup.gov.in using your application ID and password.
offlineApplication:
Method 2: Apply Offline at Sub-Registrar Office

Visit the Sub-Registrar (Vivah Panjikaran) office in the tehsil where the marriage was solemnized. Working hours: Monday – Friday, 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Locate the Correct SRO
    Go to the Sub-Registrar office (Nibandhan Karyalay) of the tehsil where the marriage was solemnized. For locations visit igrsup.gov.in.
  2. Collect Application Form
    Obtain the Marriage Registration Form (विवाह पंजीकरण फॉर्म) from the SRO counter, free of charge. You may also print it from IGRSUP portal.
  3. Fill the Form in Hindi/English
    Enter: full names (as per Aadhaar), date and place of marriage, tehsil, district, religion, and details of 2 witnesses including their Aadhaar numbers.
  4. Attach Self-Attested Copies
    Attach: age proof (10th marksheet / Birth Certificate), address proof (Aadhaar / Voter ID), individual photos of bride and groom, joint wedding photo, and ID proofs of 2 witnesses. Bring originals for counter verification.
  5. Submit & Pay
    Submit the complete form at the SRO counter. Pay ₹10 at the cash counter. Collect the Receipt / Acknowledgement.
  6. Verification & Certificate
    The Sub-Registrar verifies documents and enters details in the Vivah Register. Certificate is available for download from igrsup.gov.in using your reference number.
Helpline: IGRSUP Helpline: 0522-2308055. For late registration (over 1 year), prior approval of the District Registrar is required.
afterApplying:
After Registration - What Next?

eKYC Verification Result

Depending on Aadhaar integration success, you may or may not be called for a physical verification session at the SRO.

Download Certificate

Once approved by the Sub-Registrar, the official Marriage Certificate will be uploaded to the portal and can be retrieved using your application ID and password.
